info.name: Search Manager API
info.description:
User-facing Postman collection for Search Manager's public search, autocomplete, analytics tracking, and API-key enforcement flows.

Source: https://github.com/LindemannRock/craft-search-manager

Import the environment template, set your site URL and placeholders, then run the folders that match your setup.

item.name: Search API
item.description: Search examples for custom templates, headless integrations, and mobile/backend callers. If Require API Key is enabled, set `api_key` or `server_api_key` in the active environment.

header.description: Required for public keys that have allowed referrers.

query.description: Search query text.
One or more enabled Search Manager index handles, comma-separated. Omit on search to use the authenticated key scope or all enabled indices when allowed.
Maximum number of results to return. Search defaults to 20 and is capped at 200; autocomplete defaults to 10 and is capped at 100.

request.description: Main search endpoint. Works anonymously when Require API Key is off; requires `X-Search-Manager-Key` when Require API Key is on.

exec: pm.test('status is usable for this environment', function () {
pm.test('search response has hits array', function () {

header.description: Origin-only headless/browser test. Omit Referer so public-key referrer checks fall back to Origin.

query.description: Search query text.
One or more enabled Search Manager index handles, comma-separated. Omit on search to use the authenticated key scope or all enabled indices when allowed.
Maximum number of results to return. Search defaults to 20 and is capped at 200; autocomplete defaults to 10 and is capped at 100.

request.description: Headless browser-style search request with `Origin` and no `Referer`. When Require API Key is enabled, a public key whose allowed referrers match `origin` should authenticate.

exec: pm.test('status is usable for this environment', function () {
pm.test('origin-fallback search response has hits array', function () {

query.description: Search query text.
One or more enabled Search Manager index handles, comma-separated. Omit on search to use the authenticated key scope or all enabled indices when allowed.
Maximum number of results to return. Search defaults to 20 and is capped at 200; autocomplete defaults to 10 and is capped at 100.
When true, excludes results without a URL from the public hit list.
Snippet positioning mode: early, balanced, or deep.
Maximum snippet length in characters. Clamped to the supported snippet bounds.
When true, cleans common Markdown markers from snippet display text without changing indexed content.
When true, snippets may use block-level code from indexed page or section bodies. Inline code text is always preserved.

request.description: Mirrors the bundled widget/custom template canonical search shape.

exec: pm.test('status is usable for this environment', function () {
pm.test('canonical search response has hits array', function () {

item.name: Search - no indices uses key scope

query.description: Search query text.
Maximum number of results to return. Search defaults to 20 and is capped at 200; autocomplete defaults to 10 and is capped at 100.

request.description: When a valid key is authenticated, omitting `indexHandles` searches the key's allowed indices. For a wildcard key, that means all enabled indices.

exec: pm.test('status is usable for this environment', function () {

query.description: Search query text.
One or more enabled Search Manager index handles, comma-separated. Omit on search to use the authenticated key scope or all enabled indices when allowed.
Optional Craft site ID filter. Keyed requests must stay within the API key site/index scope.
Maximum number of results to return. Search defaults to 20 and is capped at 200; autocomplete defaults to 10 and is capped at 100.

request.description: Adds `siteId` as a site filter. Keyed requests return 400 for an unknown site and 403 when the chosen index does not cover that site.

exec: pm.test('status is usable for this environment', function () {

query.description: Search query text.
One or more enabled Search Manager index handles, comma-separated. Omit on search to use the authenticated key scope or all enabled indices when allowed.
Maximum number of results to return. Search defaults to 20 and is capped at 200; autocomplete defaults to 10 and is capped at 100.
Short analytics source identifier for this request, such as widget, backend-postman, or headless app.

request.description: Server-key example for backend-to-backend callers. Server keys skip public-key referrer checks but still enforce enabled/expired, allowed indices, max hits, site scope, and rate limit.

exec: pm.test('status is usable for this environment', function () {

item.name: Autocomplete API
item.description: Autocomplete examples for suggestions/results. Counts against per-key rate limits when Require API Key is enabled.

query.description: Search query text.
One or more enabled Search Manager index handles, comma-separated. Omit on search to use the authenticated key scope or all enabled indices when allowed.
Maximum number of results to return. Search defaults to 20 and is capped at 200; autocomplete defaults to 10 and is capped at 100.

request.description: Default autocomplete response with both term suggestions and element results.

exec: pm.test('status is usable for this environment', function () {

header.description: Origin-only headless/browser test. Omit Referer so public-key referrer checks fall back to Origin.

query.description: Search query text.
One or more enabled Search Manager index handles, comma-separated. Omit on search to use the authenticated key scope or all enabled indices when allowed.
Maximum number of results to return. Search defaults to 20 and is capped at 200; autocomplete defaults to 10 and is capped at 100.

request.description: Headless browser-style autocomplete request with `Origin` and no `Referer`. When Require API Key is enabled, a public key whose allowed referrers match `origin` should authenticate.

exec: pm.test('status is usable for this environment', function () {
pm.test('origin-fallback autocomplete response is JSON', function () {

query.description: Search query text.
One or more enabled Search Manager index handles, comma-separated. Omit on search to use the authenticated key scope or all enabled indices when allowed.
Maximum number of results to return. Search defaults to 20 and is capped at 200; autocomplete defaults to 10 and is capped at 100.
Autocomplete response selector: suggestions or results. Omit to return both.

request.description: Returns only suggestion strings.

exec: pm.test('status is usable for this environment', function () {
pm.test('suggestions-only response is an array', function () {

query.description: Search query text.
One or more enabled Search Manager index handles, comma-separated. Omit on search to use the authenticated key scope or all enabled indices when allowed.
Maximum number of results to return. Search defaults to 20 and is capped at 200; autocomplete defaults to 10 and is capped at 100.
Autocomplete response selector: suggestions or results. Omit to return both.

request.description: Returns only autocomplete result objects.

exec: pm.test('status is usable for this environment', function () {
pm.test('results-only response is an array', function () {

item.name: Analytics Tracking
item.description: Tracking endpoints used by the frontend widget. When Require API Key is enabled, they require the same key header and public-key referrer rules as search/autocomplete. Tracking is not rate-limited.

urlencoded.description: One or more enabled Search Manager index handles, comma-separated. Omit on search to use the authenticated key scope or all enabled indices when allowed.
Number of results shown to the user for this tracked search intent.
What caused the tracked search intent, such as enter, click, or idle.
Short analytics source identifier for this request, such as widget, backend-postman, or headless app.
Optional Craft site ID filter. Keyed requests must stay within the API key site/index scope.

request.description: Records a search-intent analytics row. Key attribution is stored when a valid key is authenticated.

exec: pm.test('status is usable for this environment', function () {
pm.test('track-search response has success flag', function () {

item.name: Track search intent - headless Origin fallback

header.description: Origin-only headless/browser test. Omit Referer so public-key referrer checks fall back to Origin.

urlencoded.description: One or more enabled Search Manager index handles, comma-separated. Omit on search to use the authenticated key scope or all enabled indices when allowed.
Number of results shown to the user for this tracked search intent.
What caused the tracked search intent, such as enter, click, or idle.
Short analytics source identifier for this request, such as widget, backend-postman, or headless app.
Optional Craft site ID filter. Keyed requests must stay within the API key site/index scope.

request.description: Headless browser-style tracking ping with `Origin` and no `Referer`. The origin must match `trackingAllowedOrigins` for cross-origin browser writes, and public API keys still need allowed referrers matching `origin` when Require API Key is enabled.

exec: pm.test('status is usable for this environment', function () {
pm.test('origin-fallback track-search response has success flag', function () {

request.description: CORS preflight for a trusted headless tracking origin. Expect 204 with non-wildcard CORS headers when `origin` is listed in `trackingAllowedOrigins`; otherwise expect 403.

exec: pm.test('preflight status is expected for this environment', function () {
pm.test('preflight echoes the configured origin', function () {

urlencoded.description: Element ID for a clicked result.
Single index handle for the clicked result.
One-based result position for click tracking.

request.description: Records/logs a result-click event. This endpoint is gated when Require API Key is enabled, but click persistence is intentionally log-only at this release.

exec: pm.test('status is usable for this environment', function () {
pm.test('track-click response has success flag', function () {

item.name: Enforcement Checks
item.description: Requests that help users confirm Require API Key, referrer restrictions, index scope, site scope, and rate limiting. Some requests intentionally expect failures depending on your environment.

item.name: Missing key - 200 when off, 401 when on

query.description: Search query text.
One or more enabled Search Manager index handles, comma-separated. Omit on search to use the authenticated key scope or all enabled indices when allowed.
Maximum number of results to return. Search defaults to 20 and is capped at 200; autocomplete defaults to 10 and is capped at 100.

request.description: Use this to confirm the global Require API Key setting. Expected: 200 when disabled, 401 when enabled.

exec: pm.test('missing key behavior matches one of the valid modes', function () {

item.name: Invalid key - 200 when off, 401 when on

query.description: Search query text.
One or more enabled Search Manager index handles, comma-separated. Omit on search to use the authenticated key scope or all enabled indices when allowed.
Maximum number of results to return. Search defaults to 20 and is capped at 200; autocomplete defaults to 10 and is capped at 100.

request.description: Expected: 200 when Require API Key is disabled; 401 when enabled.

exec: pm.test('invalid key behavior matches one of the valid modes', function () {

item.name: Public key bad referrer - should be 403 when restricted

query.description: Search query text.
One or more enabled Search Manager index handles, comma-separated. Omit on search to use the authenticated key scope or all enabled indices when allowed.
Maximum number of results to return. Search defaults to 20 and is capped at 200; autocomplete defaults to 10 and is capped at 100.

request.description: For a public key with allowed referrers configured, this should return 403. If the key has no referrer restriction, it may return 200.

exec: pm.test('bad referrer returns an expected status', function () {

item.name: Out-of-scope index - should be 403 for scoped keys

query.description: Search query text.
One or more enabled Search Manager index handles, comma-separated. Omit on search to use the authenticated key scope or all enabled indices when allowed.
Maximum number of results to return. Search defaults to 20 and is capped at 200; autocomplete defaults to 10 and is capped at 100.

request.description: Expected: 403 when the authenticated key does not allow `blocked_index_handle`. A wildcard/all-indices key may return 200.

exec: pm.test('out-of-scope index returns an expected status', function () {

item.name: Unknown site - should be 400 for keyed requests

query.description: Search query text.
One or more enabled Search Manager index handles, comma-separated. Omit on search to use the authenticated key scope or all enabled indices when allowed.
Optional Craft site ID filter. Keyed requests must stay within the API key site/index scope.
Maximum number of results to return. Search defaults to 20 and is capped at 200; autocomplete defaults to 10 and is capped at 100.

request.description: Expected: 400 for keyed requests when `unknown_site_id` is not a real Craft site ID. If Require API Key is off and no valid key is authenticated, anonymous traffic may return 200 with empty results.

exec: pm.test('unknown site returns an expected status', function () {

item.name: Rate limit probe - repeat with Runner

query.description: Search query text.
One or more enabled Search Manager index handles, comma-separated. Omit on search to use the authenticated key scope or all enabled indices when allowed.
Maximum number of results to return. Search defaults to 20 and is capped at 200; autocomplete defaults to 10 and is capped at 100.
When true, search returns results without recording search analytics.

request.description: Use Postman Runner to repeat this request more times than the key's per-minute rate limit. Expected: 429 after the cap. Tracking requests do not count toward this cap.

exec: pm.test('rate-limit probe returns an expected status', function () {
